GenoSim 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

Simulate Diploid SNP Genotype Data Across Generations (Population Mode)

Source: R/simulate_genotypes.R
simulate_population.Rd

Performs forward-time simulation of diploid biallelic SNP genotypes for a panmictic population over up to 10 generations. Biological processes modelled include Hardy-Weinberg genotype sampling, Wright-Fisher genetic drift, additive directional selection, per-locus mutation, and recombination-aware gamete transmission. Inbreeding is implemented via the standard \(F\)-coefficient deflation of heterozygosity.

simulate_population(
  n_founders = 100,
  n_snps = 1000,
  n_generations = 5,
  inbreeding_F = 0,
  mut_rate = 1e-04,
  selection_s = 0,
  maf_min = 0.05,
  n_eff = NULL,
  n_offspring_per_couple = 3,
  chromosomes = 1:22,
  seed = 42,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

n_founders

Integer. Number of founder individuals. Must be \(\ge 10\).

n_snps

Integer. Number of biallelic SNPs to simulate. Range 1???100,000.

n_generations

Integer. Number of generations to simulate. Range 1???10.

inbreeding_F

Numeric in \([0, 1)\). Wright's inbreeding coefficient \(F\). A value of 0 gives Hardy-Weinberg proportions; 0.125 approximates a first-cousin mating system; 0.25 approximates half-sibling matings.

mut_rate

Numeric in \([0, 0.01]\). Per-locus per-generation mutation rate (bidirectional: \(A \to a\) and \(a \to A\)).

selection_s

Numeric in \([-1, 1]\). Additive selection coefficient. Positive values favour the alternative allele; 0 gives neutral evolution.

maf_min

Numeric in \((0, 0.5]\). Minimum minor allele frequency assigned to founder SNPs. Founder allele frequencies are drawn uniformly from [maf_min, 0.5].

n_eff

Integer or NULL. Effective population size \(N_e\) governing genetic drift intensity. Defaults to n_founders.

n_offspring_per_couple

Integer \(\ge 2\). Number of offspring produced per mating pair per generation.

chromosomes

Integer vector. Autosome numbers (subset of 1???22) across which SNPs are distributed proportionally to chromosome length.

seed

Integer or NULL. Random seed for reproducibility.

verbose

Logical. Whether to print progress messages. Default TRUE.

Value

A named list with the following elements:

genotypes

Named list of dosage matrices (0/1/2) per generation. Each matrix is \(n_{indiv} \times n_{SNPs}\), with rownames gen0_ind1, gen1_ind1, etc.

snp_map

A data.frame with columns snp_id, chrom, pos_bp, and founder_maf.

allele_freqs

Matrix of alternative allele frequencies, dimensions \((n_{generations}+1) \times n_{SNPs}\).

summary_stats

A data.frame of per-generation statistics: observed/expected heterozygosity, \(F_{IS}\), mean MAF, fraction of fixed loci, and mean dosage.

params

List of all input parameters used.

Details

Genotype model: Under inbreeding coefficient \(F\), genotype probabilities are: $$P(AA) = p^2(1-F) + pF$$ $$P(Aa) = 2pq(1-F)$$ $$P(aa) = q^2(1-F) + qF$$

Drift: Each generation, allele frequencies are updated via a Binomial draw \(Bin(2N_e, p)\) (Wright-Fisher model).

Selection: Additive model with fitness \(w_{AA}=1+s\), \(w_{Aa}=1+s/2\), \(w_{aa}=1\).

Recombination: Haldane mapping function applied between adjacent SNPs on each chromosome, with default rate \(10^{-8}\) per bp per meiosis.

References

Wright S (1931) Evolution in Mendelian populations. Genetics 16:97???159.

See also

simulate_from_pedigree for pedigree-constrained simulation, hwe_test, compute_ld, run_pca, plot_dashboard, export_vcf

Examples

# Basic neutral simulation
sim <- simulate_population(
  n_founders    = 100,
  n_snps        = 500,
  n_generations = 5,
  seed          = 42
)
#> === GenoSim: Population Forward Simulator ===
#>   Founders: 100 | SNPs: 500 | Generations: 5
#>   F=0.000 | mu=1.00e-04 | s=0.000 | Ne=100
#> [1/4] Simulating founder genotypes...
#> [2/4] Running forward simulation...
#>   -> Generation 1/5
#>   -> Generation 2/5
#>   -> Generation 3/5
#>   -> Generation 4/5
#>   -> Generation 5/5
#> [3/4] Computing summary statistics...
#> 
#> === Simulation Complete ===
#>   Total individuals: 2071 | SNPs: 500
# Inspect summary statistics
sim$summary_stats
#>   generation n_individuals n_snps obs_heterozygosity exp_heterozygosity
#> 1          0           100    500            0.36076            0.36219
#> 2          1           150    500            0.36352            0.36024
#> 3          2           225    500            0.36076            0.35760
#> 4          3           336    500            0.36094            0.35846
#> 5          4           504    500            0.35790            0.35590
#> 6          5           756    500            0.36181            0.35780
#>   inbreeding_fis mean_maf frac_fixed mean_dosage
#> 1        0.00396  0.27174          0     1.04104
#> 2       -0.00910  0.27211          0     1.03747
#> 3       -0.00884  0.26910          0     1.03706
#> 4       -0.00692  0.26930          0     1.03508
#> 5       -0.00563  0.26722          0     1.03549
#> 6       -0.01121  0.26913          0     1.03667

# High-inbreeding scenario (first-cousin system)
sim_inbred <- simulate_population(
  n_founders    = 80,
  n_snps        = 1000,
  n_generations = 5,
  inbreeding_F  = 0.125,
  n_eff         = 60,
  chromosomes   = 1:5,
  seed          = 2024
)
#> === GenoSim: Population Forward Simulator ===
#>   Founders: 80 | SNPs: 1000 | Generations: 5
#>   F=0.125 | mu=1.00e-04 | s=0.000 | Ne=60
#> [1/4] Simulating founder genotypes...
#> [2/4] Running forward simulation...
#>   -> Generation 1/5
#>   -> Generation 2/5
#>   -> Generation 3/5
#>   -> Generation 4/5
#>   -> Generation 5/5
#> [3/4] Computing summary statistics...
#> 
#> === Simulation Complete ===
#>   Total individuals: 1661 | SNPs: 1000

# Positive selection scenario
sim_sel <- simulate_population(
  n_founders    = 200,
  n_snps        = 2000,
  n_generations = 8,
  selection_s   = 0.05,
  seed          = 999
)
#> === GenoSim: Population Forward Simulator ===
#>   Founders: 200 | SNPs: 2000 | Generations: 8
#>   F=0.000 | mu=1.00e-04 | s=0.050 | Ne=200
#> [1/4] Simulating founder genotypes...
#> [2/4] Running forward simulation...
#>   -> Generation 1/8
#>   -> Generation 2/8
#>   -> Generation 3/8
#>   -> Generation 4/8
#>   -> Generation 5/8
#>   -> Generation 6/8
#>   -> Generation 7/8
#>   -> Generation 8/8
#> [3/4] Computing summary statistics...
#> 
#> === Simulation Complete ===
#>   Total individuals: 14933 | SNPs: 2000
